title: Mnemosyne Reflexion Lesson
description: One durable lesson in a Mnemosyne reflexion memory. JSONL is the source of truth; LESSONS.md is a generated human view. Authored only via the engine (never hand-edited). Domain-specific enums (categories, stages, and the trigger axes) are defined by the active mnemosyne.config.json and enforced by `mnemosyne validate`, not by this schema.

id.description: Stable zero-padded id, e.g. L-0007. The prefix is set by config.id_prefix. Assigned by the engine; never reused.

title.description: Short imperative summary of the lesson, e.g. 'Always pin idempotency for new POST endpoints'.

category.description: Topical axis (config.categories). Default set: mistake=learned-from-failure; decision=reusable ruling; convention=style/naming/layering; security=authz/PII/audit; architecture=structural; process=how the work itself should run.

memory_type.description: Cognitive-memory axis (config.memory_types). episodic=a reflection tied to a specific past event; semantic=a consolidated standing fact/decision; procedural=a rule that changes how the work is run.

lesson.description: The actionable guidance to apply next time. 1-3 sentences. Says what to DO, not just what went wrong.

rationale.description: Why this lesson exists: the evidence, the failure it prevents, or the decision's justification.

trigger.description: When this lesson applies. recall() scores a lesson by how well these fields overlap the current context. Every key is a config axis (plus 'stages'); all optional. An empty trigger means 'always consider'. tags carry the most lexical weight.

stages.description: Lifecycle stage(s) this lesson should be recalled in (config.stages). recall --stage boosts a lesson listing the current stage. Empty = every stage.

tags.description: Free-text keywords for lexical match, e.g. ['idempotency','transaction','pii']. Usually the primary recall signal.

source.description: Provenance, so a reviewer can verify the lesson against its origin.

anchors.description: file:line or url anchors backing the lesson.

reflection_of.description: For episodic lessons: the event/feedback that triggered the reflection (what went wrong).

stage.description: Lifecycle stage this lesson was learned in (provenance; config.stages).

confidence.description: How strongly to weight this lesson (config.confidence). Low-confidence lessons are surfaced but flagged for confirmation.

status.description: active=in play; superseded=replaced by a newer lesson; retired=no longer applies (kept for audit).

superseded_by.description: If superseded, the id that replaced it.

related.description: Related lesson ids (rendered as [[L-xxxx]] links in LESSONS.md).

review.description: Promotion/governance state. New lessons start 'local' (per-developer, gitignored). 'proposed' = a PR is open. 'approved' = merged into the shared store.

uses.description: Baseline recall count as authored/seeded. Live recall usage is tracked in the gitignored memory/usage.local.json sidecar so reads never churn this committed file; stats/prune merge the two.

reinforced.description: How many times a near-duplicate capture reinforced this lesson instead of adding a new record. High reinforced = repeatedly re-learned; can earn higher confidence and protects against pruning.

last_used.description: ISO date this lesson was last reinforced (set by dedup-on-capture). A recency signal used by prune.

retired_reason.description: If status=retired by `mnemosyne prune`, the machine-written reason. The record is KEPT for audit, never deleted.
